mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index signals expanding output and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days. The mechanism is always active as a trend-following signal based on the level of INDPRO relative to its 12-month moving average.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of periods when INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This threshold trigger is activated only when the level exceeds the threshold.
failure_condition: After UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, the S&P 500 delivers positive returns in more than 50% of subsequent 20-day periods over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Low real federal funds rate (nominal rate minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) indicates accommodative monetary policy that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations. The signal is active when the real rate is below 2%, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with Real FFR < 2%, the S&P 500 60-day return is negative in more than 50% of instances over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a housing start level above its 12-month moving average signals overbuilding and potential economic overheating, which precedes bearish equity returns. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle expansion (UNRATE < 5% and FEDFUNDS > 4%), rising HOUST above MA is followed by positive 5-day returns more than 60% of the time over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and boosts S&P 500 returns. The mechanism is always active: the level of RSAFS relative to its 12-month moving average provides a persistent trend signal for the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of periods when RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5.0%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that slows economic activity and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The threshold trigger activates only above this level.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, the S&P 500 delivers positive returns in more than 50% of subsequent 20-day periods over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in both employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) above their 12-month moving averages signals a broad-based economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This interaction-driven signal requires both series to be in expansionary territory.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and INDPRO above their respective 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO are above their 12-month MAs, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rapid acceleration in CPI inflation (year-over-year change exceeding 3%) intensifies the risk of further tightening and erodes real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is regime-conditional on the monetary policy stance.
failure_condition: In a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising and above 4%), when CPI YoY exceeds 3%, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances over any 5-year window.
